title: Australia Faces New Challenge as Bird Flu Reaches Mainland
subtitle: The arrival of the H5N1 virus in Western Australia marks the end of the continent's status as the last region free from the deadly disease.

summary.en: Australia has officially confirmed its first cases of the H5N1 bird flu on the mainland. A sick brown skua found in a national park tested positive for the virus and later died. Experts are now working to monitor the situation and determine if the disease has spread to other animals. This virus has already caused massive damage to wildlife populations across the globe since 2021. Authorities are focusing on protecting local wildlife and the poultry industry from this new threat.
summary.zh: 澳洲已正式確認本土出現首批 H5N1 禽流感病例。一隻在國家公園發現的病鳥「褐賊鷗」經檢測呈陽性後死亡。專家目前正致力於監測情況，並判定該疾病是否已傳播至其他動物。自 2021 年以來，此病毒已對全球野生動物族群造成巨大破壞。當局正聚焦於保護當地野生動物與家禽產業，以應對這項新威脅。

paragraphs.en: Australia has officially confirmed the arrival of the H5N1 bird flu on its mainland, marking a significant change for the country. For a long time, Australia was the only continent in the world that remained free of this dangerous virus. However, federal agriculture minister Julie Collins confirmed this weekend that a brown skua, a type of wild migratory bird, died after testing positive for the H5N1 virus in Western Australia. A second bird, a giant petrel found in the same area, has also tested positive for the disease. These findings have raised serious concerns among scientists and government officials.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲已正式確認 H5N1 禽流感病毒傳入其本土，這對該國而言是一個重大的轉變。長期以來，澳洲是全球唯一未受此危險病毒侵襲的大陸。然而，聯邦農業部長柯林斯 (Julie Collins) 於本週末證實，一隻在西澳發現的野生候鳥「褐賊鷗」在檢測出 H5N1 病毒陽性後死亡。另一隻在同一地區發現的「巨海燕」也對該疾病呈現陽性反應。這些發現已引起科學家與政府官員的高度擔憂。

paragraphs.en: The H5N1 virus is described by experts as a "highly pathogenic" virus. This means it is very easy to spread and causes serious illness or death in animals. According to The Guardian, the virus has already killed millions of birds and thousands of marine mammals since it began spreading across the world in 2021. Dr. Ariful Islam, a veterinary epidemiologist at Charles Sturt University, calls the situation a "panzootic," which means the disease has moved across multiple continents and affected many different species. Wildlife Health Australia reports that more than 560 wild bird species and over 100 types of mammals have been affected by the virus so far.
paragraphs.zh: 專家將 H5N1 病毒描述為「高病原性」病毒，這意味著它極易傳播，並會導致動物嚴重患病或死亡。根據《衛報》報導，自 2021 年該病毒開始在全球擴散以來，已造成數百萬隻鳥類與數千隻海洋哺乳動物死亡。查爾斯斯特大學 (Charles Sturt University) 的獸醫流行病學家阿里夫·伊斯蘭博士 (Dr. Ariful Islam) 將此情況稱為「泛動物疫病」，意指該疾病已跨越多個大陸並影響了許多不同物種。澳洲野生動物健康組織 (Wildlife Health Australia) 指出，迄今已有超過 560 種野生鳥類與 100 多種哺乳動物受到該病毒影響。

paragraphs.en: Researchers believe the virus reached Australian territory as early as August 2025, when it was found on Heard Island, a remote area located thousands of kilometers from the mainland. Since then, the virus has caused significant harm to local wildlife, including penguins and seals. According to BirdLife Australia, the two birds recently found on the mainland most likely traveled from the subantarctic region. Scientists are now working to find out if the virus has spread further into the local environment. Dr. Islam noted that more environmental testing is needed to understand how the disease is moving through the area.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人員認為，該病毒早在 2025 年 8 月就已抵達澳洲領土，當時是在距離本土數千公里遠的偏遠地區「赫德島」發現的。自那時起，該病毒已對當地的野生動物（包括企鵝與海豹）造成嚴重傷害。根據澳洲鳥盟 (BirdLife Australia) 的說法，近期在本土發現的兩隻鳥類極有可能是從亞南極地區遷徙而來。科學家目前正致力於調查病毒是否已進一步擴散至當地環境。伊斯蘭博士指出，需要進行更多的環境檢測，以了解該疾病在該地區的傳播路徑。

paragraphs.en: For the Australian government, the immediate priority is to stop the virus from causing a larger disaster. Minister Julie Collins stated that there is currently no evidence of mass deaths or infections among poultry, which are birds raised for food like chickens. The government is starting a coordinated national response to monitor wildlife populations. This effort will help officials decide what steps to take next to protect both nature and the farming industry. Experts believe that more information about the extent of the outbreak will be available within the coming days.
paragraphs.zh: 對澳洲政府而言，當務之急是防止病毒引發更大規模的災難。柯林斯部長表示，目前沒有證據顯示作為食用禽類的家禽（如雞隻）出現大規模死亡或感染。政府正啟動一項協調性的全國應對措施，以監測野生動物族群。這項工作將有助於官員決定下一步該採取何種措施，以保護自然生態與農業產業。專家預計，未來幾天內將會有更多關於疫情規模的資訊。

paragraphs.en: This situation is different from the H7 bird flu outbreaks that affected poultry farms in several Australian states during 2024 and 2025. Those earlier outbreaks led to the loss of hundreds of thousands of chickens. Dr. Michelle Wille, an expert at the University of Melbourne, explained that H5 bird flu has historically had a very serious impact on poultry industries in other parts of the world. Because of this, the agricultural sector is watching the situation very closely. The arrival of the virus is a major test for Australia's biosecurity systems, which are the measures taken to protect the country from harmful diseases.
paragraphs.zh: 此情況與 2024 年至 2025 年間影響澳洲多個州的 H7 禽流感疫情不同。先前的疫情導致數十萬隻雞隻損失。墨爾本大學 (University of Melbourne) 的專家威爾博士 (Dr. Michelle Wille) 解釋說，H5 禽流感在歷史上對世界其他地區的家禽產業造成了極其嚴重的衝擊。正因如此，農業部門正密切關注事態發展。此次病毒的入侵，對澳洲的生物安全系統（即為保護國家免受有害疾病侵害所採取的措施）是一項重大考驗。

paragraphs.en: As the investigation continues, the focus remains on gathering data and preventing the virus from reaching farm animals. The government is working with the CSIRO’s Australian Centre for Disease Preparedness to confirm test results and track the virus. While the arrival of H5N1 is a worrying development, officials are urging the public to remain calm while they work to manage the risks. The coming weeks will be critical in determining whether the virus can be contained or if it will become a permanent part of the Australian landscape. For now, the main goal is to protect the country's unique wildlife and ensure that the poultry industry remains safe from this global health challenge.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著調查持續進行，重點仍放在收集數據並防止病毒傳播至農場動物。政府正與聯邦科學與工業研究組織 (CSIRO) 下屬的澳洲疾病準備中心合作，以確認檢測結果並追蹤病毒。儘管 H5N1 的出現是一項令人擔憂的發展，官員仍呼籲大眾在他們致力於控管風險的同時保持冷靜。未來幾週將是關鍵，以確定該病毒是否能被圍堵，或是將成為澳洲環境中永久存在的一部分。目前，主要目標是保護該國獨特的野生動物，並確保家禽產業免受這項全球健康挑戰的威脅。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To breed or keep animals for a specific purpose, such as food.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見意思為「舉起」或「提高」，這裡指飼養家禽。文中：Minister Julie Collins stated that there is currently no evidence of mass deaths or infections among poultry, which are birds raised for food like chickens.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A difficult situation that shows how strong or effective something is.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見意思為「測驗」或「檢測」，這裡指對系統能力的考驗。文中：The arrival of the virus is a major test for Australia's biosecurity systems, which are the measures taken to protect the country from harmful diseases.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To follow the progress or development of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見意思為「軌道」或「足跡」，這裡作動詞指追蹤病毒動向。文中：The government is working with the CSIRO’s Australian Centre for Disease Preparedness to confirm test results and track the virus.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: ‘A genuine wildlife emergency’: everything you need to know about the arrival of H5 bird flu in Australia

previousArticles.title: Australia Confirms First Mainland Case of H5N1 Bird Flu
previousArticles.summaryEn: Australia has officially confirmed its first mainland case of the H5N1 bird flu virus. The disease was found in a brown skua at a national park in Western Australia. While the virus has caused significant harm to wildlife globally, officials report no signs of mass deaths in the country yet. The government has invested millions in preparation and is currently monitoring the situation closely. Authorities are urging the public to avoid contact with sick or dead birds.

quiz.question: Which specific birds were confirmed to have tested positive for H5N1 on the Australian mainland?

options.A: A penguin and a seal found on Heard Island.
options.B: A brown skua and a giant petrel found in Western Australia.
options.C: A group of chickens from a local poultry farm.
options.D: Two migratory birds that were found dead in the subantarctic region.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，聯邦農業部長 Julie Collins 確認在澳洲西部（Western Australia）發現一隻棕賊鷗（brown skua）與一隻巨海燕（giant petrel）對 H5N1 檢測呈陽性。選項 A 提到的企鵝與海豹是在 Heard Island 被發現，而非澳洲大陸；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章第四段提到目前沒有證據顯示家禽受感染；選項 D 雖然提到這些鳥類來自亞南極地區，但發現地點是在澳洲大陸而非亞南極。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why are Australian officials particularly concerned about the arrival of H5N1 compared to the H7 outbreaks in 2024-2025?

options.A: Because H5N1 is known to be much easier to cure than H7.
options.B: Because H7 only affected wild animals, while H5N1 is limited to poultry.
options.C: Because the global history of H5N1 suggests it poses a more severe threat to both wildlife and the poultry industry.
options.D: Because the government had no biosecurity systems in place before the H5N1 arrival.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，專家指出 H5N1 在全球範圍內對家禽業造成了嚴重影響，且該病毒已造成數百萬隻鳥類與數千隻海洋哺乳動物死亡。雖然澳洲曾有 H7 疫情，但 H5N1 的全球傳播力與致死率使其成為對澳洲生物安全系統的重大考驗。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提及 H5N1 較易治療；選項 B 與事實相反，H5N1 影響野生動物與家禽；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到政府已有生物安全系統，此次事件是對該系統的測試。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, what does the term 'panzootic' imply about the H5N1 virus?

options.A: It is a virus that only affects a single species of bird.
options.B: It is a disease that has spread across multiple continents and affects many species.
options.C: It is a virus that has been successfully contained in one specific region.
options.D: It is a medical term for a disease that is only found in laboratory settings.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Dr. Ariful Islam 將 H5N1 稱為「panzootic」，並緊接著解釋這意味著該疾病已跨越多個大陸並影響了許多不同的物種。選項 A 與定義矛盾；選項 C 錯誤，因為 panzootic 強調的是廣泛傳播而非受控；選項 D 錯誤，這是一個描述動物流行病學的術語，並非侷限於實驗室。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of the Australian government following the detection of H5N1 on the mainland?

options.A: To immediately cull all wild bird populations to prevent further spread.
options.B: To blame international travelers for bringing the virus into the country.
options.C: To confirm the virus has already reached all major poultry farms.
options.D: To monitor wildlife and protect the poultry industry while gathering more data.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章第四段與第六段總結了政府的優先事項：啟動協調性的國家應對措施以監測野生動物，並致力於防止病毒傳播至農場動物，同時收集數據以評估疫情範圍。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提到撲殺野生鳥類；選項 B 文中未提及；選項 C 錯誤，目前政府強調的是防止病毒傳播至家禽業，而非確認其已遍布農場。
